retry_reason: No failure path exists; the reference operations (get/put) are atomic and always succeed when called on valid labels (they are assumed to be alive).
contract_summary: This function clones the AppArmor security context from a parent socket to a child socket during socket cloning. For each of the three label fields (label, peer, peer_lastupdate), it releases any existing reference in the child and acquires a new reference to the parent's label using aa_get_label_rcu, ensuring proper reference counting. The child socket's security context becomes a shallow copy with incremented refcounts. The caller must ensure the child socket's context is not concurrently modified during this operation and must eventually release the child's labels (e.g., via aa_put_label) when the socket is freed. If the parent and child already share the same pointer for a field, no refcount change occurs, preserving efficiency.
